**Pascal "Panzer" Krauss** (born April 19, 1987) is a retired German [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") who competed in the [welterweight](/wiki/Welterweight_(MMA) "Welterweight (MMA)") division of the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") (UFC). His German nickname translates to 'tank' in English.

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pascal_Krauss&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Krauss was born on April 19, 1987, in [Breisach](/wiki/Breisach "Breisach"), [West Germany](/wiki/West_Germany "West Germany"). He developed an interest for [martial arts](/wiki/Martial_arts "Martial arts") was woken when he started [boxing](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing") at the age of 14. His five-year commitment to the sport earned him a German junior boxing title and a second place in the International German Championships. The latter being the only loss in his 18-fight amateur boxing career. He was also a member in the youth team of  [Freiburg's](/wiki/Freiburg "Freiburg") first division [wrestling](/wiki/Wrestling "Wrestling") club 'RKG Freiburg 2000'. Krauss studied sport science at the [University of Freiburg](/wiki/University_of_Freiburg "University of Freiburg").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pascal_Krauss&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### Mixed martial arts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pascal_Krauss&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts")]

After an encounter with German [Judo](/wiki/Judo "Judo") [black belt](/wiki/Black_belt_(martial_arts) "Black belt (martial arts)") and MMA practioneer Gregor Herb [[3]](#cite_note-3) in 2007 Pascal also started training [Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu](/wiki/Brazilian_Jiu-Jitsu "Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu") (BJJ) and MMA. He made his professional MMA debut in January 2008 in his native Germany.

In 2009, Pascal spent some time in [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), Brazil, to train under BJJ master Roberto “Gordo” Correa. After eight weeks of training alongside [Antonio Braga Neto](/wiki/Antonio_Braga_Neto "Antonio Braga Neto"), [Rafael dos Anjos](/wiki/Rafael_dos_Anjos "Rafael dos Anjos"), Delson Heleno and MMA-legend [Vitor Belfort](/wiki/Vitor_Belfort "Vitor Belfort"), amongst others, he was awarded a BJJ blue belt. A 2010 trip to the United States lead Pascal to spend some time at  [Renzo Gracie's](/wiki/Renzo_Gracie "Renzo Gracie") MMA academy in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") and the Cesar Gracie MMA team [[4]](#cite_note-4) - home to renowned fighters such as [Jake Shields](/wiki/Jake_Shields "Jake Shields"), [Nick Diaz](/wiki/Nick_Diaz "Nick Diaz"), [Nate Diaz](/wiki/Nate_Diaz "Nate Diaz") and [Gilbert Melendez](/wiki/Gilbert_Melendez "Gilbert Melendez").

After beating John Quinn at the May 22, 2010 [Cage Warriors](/wiki/Cage_Warriors "Cage Warriors") Fighting Championship *Right to Fight* event[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) he became the Cage Warriors welterweight champion.[[7]](#cite_note-7) He succeeded [Dan Hardy](/wiki/Dan_Hardy "Dan Hardy"), who had to vacate the title after signing with the UFC in 2008.

The MMA-website [Sherdog](/wiki/Sherdog "Sherdog") released an interview with Pascal on July 24 for their "Prospect Watch"-column.[[8]](#cite_note-8) Sherdog also named him in a July 2010 article on 'the top 10 undefeated European prospects'.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

#### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pascal_Krauss&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

In August 2010, Pascal signed a four-fight contract with the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship").[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11) He was expected to make his promotional debut at [UFC 122](/wiki/UFC_122 "UFC 122") in [Oberhausen](/wiki/Oberhausen "Oberhausen"), Germany against the likewise undefeated [Kenny Robertson](/wiki/Kenny_Robertson "Kenny Robertson").[[12]](#cite_note-12) In mid October, Robertson backed out of the fight due to injury and was replaced by fellow undefeated UFC newcomer Mark Scanlon.[[13]](#cite_note-13) Krauss won via unanimous decision.

Krauss was expected to face [John Hathaway](/wiki/John_Hathaway "John Hathaway") on November 5, 2011, at [UFC 138](/wiki/UFC_138 "UFC 138").[[14]](#cite_note-14) However, Krauss pulled out of the bout citing a shoulder injury, and was replaced by [Matt Brown](/wiki/Matt_Brown_(fighter) "Matt Brown (fighter)").[[15]](#cite_note-15)

Krauss/Hathaway eventually took place on May 5, 2012, at [UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3").[[16]](#cite_note-16) Krauss lost the fight via unanimous decision.

Krauss was expected to face UFC newcomer [Gunnar Nelson](/wiki/Gunnar_Nelson_(fighter) "Gunnar Nelson (fighter)") on September 29, 2012, at [UFC on Fuel TV 5](/wiki/UFC_on_Fuel_TV_5 "UFC on Fuel TV 5").[[17]](#cite_note-17) However, Krauss was forced to pull out of the fight after he received a strong knee strike to the ribcage while sparring and had to be rushed to the hospital after complaining of chest pain. Doctors at Freiburg University Hospital diagnosed him with severely bruised ribs, an inflamed chest muscle and a dislocated vertebra, rendering Krauss unable to compete and was replaced on the card by [DaMarques Johnson](/wiki/DaMarques_Johnson "DaMarques Johnson").[[18]](#cite_note-JohnsonIn-18)

Krauss faced Mike Stumpf on January 26, 2013, at [UFC on Fox 6](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_6 "UFC on Fox 6").[[19]](#cite_note-19) He won the fight via unanimous decision.

Krauss faced [Hyun Gyu Lim](/wiki/Hyun_Gyu_Lim "Hyun Gyu Lim") on August 31, 2013, at [UFC 164](/wiki/UFC_164 "UFC 164").[[20]](#cite_note-20) He lost by KO in the first round. Despite the loss, the fight earned Krauss his second *Fight of the Night* bonus award.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

Krauss was expected to face promotional newcomer Adam Khaliev on January 25, 2014, at [UFC on Fox 10](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_10 "UFC on Fox 10").[[22]](#cite_note-22) However, the bout was scrapped in the weeks leading up to the event as Khaliev had not been granted clearance for the fight.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

## Championships and accomplishments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pascal_Krauss&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Championships and accomplishments")]

* **[Cage Warriors](/wiki/Cage_Warriors "Cage Warriors")**
  + Cage Warriors Welterweight Championship (One time)
* **[Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship")**
  + [Fight of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") (Two times)
